column1: # NEW FILE (Alfanumrik original) — follows the rubric CSV schema and P/R/O/M bucket
# framework of "Agent Skills for K-12 Teachers" (https://github.com/anthropics/
# No criterion text is copied from upstream. Scores EXISTING quiz-generator output
# batches. The QZ-P6* criteria are derived DIRECTLY from product invariant P6
# (.claude/CLAUDE.md) and mirror the semantics of the REG-54 validation oracle
# the canonical STRING difficulty enum easy|medium|hard (A3). Criteria marked
# "Deterministic pre-check" in Notes are enforced mechanically before the LLM judge
# (REG-54 oracle pattern) and are NOT sent to the judge.
# QZ-P6f sampling boundary: the string difficulty enum easy|medium|hard applies to
# quiz-oracle boundary) — NOT to the generator's *served* responses
# question_bank's INTEGER difficulty 1|2|3; grading served batches requires a documented
# integer->string normalization first. Also note QZ-P6f validates difficulty/bloom_level
# only when the fields are present — a difficulty-less batch PASS is not full P6
# Lines starting with '#' are comments (harness parser skips them).

column4: Every question in the batch has non-empty question_text containing no template residue: no '{{' and no '[BLANK]' marker. Fail = any question with empty text or a placeholder marker.
column5: Product invariant P6. Deterministic pre-check: enforced mechanically before the LLM judge (mirrors quiz-oracle p6_text_empty_or_placeholder).

column4: Every question offers exactly 4 answer options. Fail = any question with fewer or more than 4 options or a non-array options field.
column5: Product invariant P6. Deterministic pre-check (mirrors quiz-oracle p6_options_not_4).

column4: Every question's 4 options are all non-empty strings and all distinct (case-insensitive, after trimming). Fail = any empty option or any case-insensitive duplicate.
column5: Product invariant P6. Deterministic pre-check (mirrors quiz-oracle p6_options_not_4 / p6_options_not_distinct).

column4: Every question's correct_answer_index is an integer between 0 and 3 inclusive. Fail = missing / non-integer / out-of-range index.
column5: Product invariant P6. Deterministic pre-check (mirrors quiz-oracle p6_correct_index_out_of_range).

column4: Every question carries a non-empty explanation. Fail = any question with an empty or missing explanation.
column5: Product invariant P6. Deterministic pre-check (mirrors quiz-oracle p6_explanation_empty).

column4: Every question's difficulty, when present, is one of the canonical strings easy|medium|hard, and its bloom_level, when present, is one of remember|understand|apply|analyze|evaluate|create. Fail = any other value.
column5: Product invariant P6. Deterministic pre-check (mirrors quiz-oracle p6_invalid_difficulty / p6_invalid_bloom; string difficulty enum per A3).

column3: Grade is a string in CBSE band (P5)
column4: The batch (and any per-question grade echo) records grade as a JSON string "6" through "12" — never an integer. Fail = integer grade, missing batch grade, or out-of-band value.
column5: Product invariant P5. Deterministic pre-check (mirrors quiz-oracle p5_invalid_grade).

column3: Within CBSE scope for grade and subject
column4: Every question tests content that belongs to the CBSE syllabus for the batch's stated grade and subject. Fail = any question drawing on out-of-syllabus or wrong-grade content.
column5: Product invariant P12 — curriculum scope. LLM-judged.

column4: For every question, the three wrong options are plausible: each reflects a believable error a student at this grade could make on this concept (a computational slip, a common misconception, a near-miss definition) — not absurd, obviously-joke, or off-topic fillers. Name any implausible distractor in the explanation.
column5: Distractor quality — the discriminating power of the item. LLM-judged.

column4: For every question, the explanation actually explains why the option at correct_answer_index is correct (and, ideally, why tempting distractors are wrong). Fail = an explanation that contradicts the keyed answer, justifies a different option, or merely restates the question.
column5: Catches key/explanation mismatch — the failure mode the REG-54 LLM grader targets. LLM-judged.

column4: Every question and explanation is worded age-appropriately for the batch's grade band (6-8 / 9-10 / 11-12): vocabulary and sentence complexity match the band, and no content is inappropriate for school-age students. Fail = any question a teacher would not put in front of that class.
